DTR | Listen to a Speaker Support an Opinion
LSO-MS8-L2-1400P-MSMATH8-RWS-LAT-01 | MS Math: Grade 8 | Distance Between Points on the Coordinate Plane | Real-World Simulation | Latinx / Latin American Experiences
Cutting Across the Square
Click 🎧 to listen.
Listen to a conversation between two students. The girl is discussing her opinion about how to measure distance when planning a festival map. Listen carefully. You will hear the conversation only once. After listening, you will answer four questions about the conversation and the evidence the girl gives to support her opinion. As you listen, you may take notes.
Listen to the Conversation
1QUESTION 1:
Why does the girl mention what happened last year?
2QUESTION 2:
Where did the girl first learn the way to measure the real straight distance?
3QUESTION 3:
What does the girl say about the two ways of measuring the distance to the stage?
4QUESTION 4:
What is the girl's main reason that measuring the real straight distance matters?
Answer all 4 questions to submit.
Choose the best answer for each question. You may change your answers before submitting.
SMART SUPPORT:
Go back to the conversation. What is the girl trying to accomplish across the whole conversation, and how does her choice to compare ten squares across with fourteen along the rows shape what she concludes about measuring distance?
CA ELD: PI.A.3, PI.B.5, PI.B.7, PI.B.8, PII.A.1 | CCSS: 8.G.B.8 | MS Math: Grade 8
